title: Training Modality
description: A category of physical quality. Goal profiles weight these; the scheduler uses recovery cost and compatibility rules to place sessions. One file per modality.

id.description: Unique slug matching the enum used in other schemas. e.g. 'aerobic_base', 'max_strength'

description.description: One-sentence definition. e.g. 'Zone 1-2 steady-state work building the aerobic engine.'

recovery_cost.description: How demanding this modality is on recovery. Governs minimum rest before repeating. low = can stack on adjacent days; medium = 24h; high = 48h minimum.

recovery_hours_min.description: Minimum hours before this modality can be repeated or paired with another high-cost modality.

session_position.description: Where in a session this modality should appear when paired with others. 'standalone' means it should not be combined.

compatible_in_session_with.description: Modality IDs that can share a session with this one. Order matters — list higher-priority pairings first.

incompatible_in_session_with.description: Modality IDs that must not share a session with this one.

min_weekly_minutes.description: Minimum effective weekly volume in minutes. Below this, adaptation does not occur.

max_weekly_minutes.description: Maximum recoverable weekly volume in minutes. Above this, performance degrades.

typical_session_minutes.description: Typical session length range in minutes.

progression_model.description: ID of the primary progression model that governs advancement in this modality.

intensity_zones.description: Intensity levels meaningful for this modality. Not all modalities use heart-rate zones.
